exercises.title: How many calls pass when bulkhead is full?
exercises.instructions: Config: maxConcurrentCalls=3, maxWaitDuration=0ms. 5 concurrent calls arrive. How many proceed? How many get BulkheadFullException? What does the fallback return?
exercises.expected_solution:
3 calls: acquire semaphore permits (maxConcurrentCalls=3) → proceed normally.
2 calls: no permits available, maxWaitDuration=0ms → wait 0ms → BulkheadFullException.
Fallback called for the 2 rejected calls.
When any of the 3 active calls complete → next waiting request (if any) can proceed.

test_cases.expected:
3 calls: acquire semaphore permits (maxConcurrentCalls=3) → proceed normally.
2 calls: no permits available, maxWaitDuration=0ms → wait 0ms → BulkheadFullException.
Fallback called for the 2 rejected calls.
When any of the 3 active calls complete → next waiting request (if any) can proceed.

hints: maxConcurrentCalls: semaphore permits available
0ms wait: fail immediately when full
When a call completes: it releases a permit

exercises.title: Complete @Bulkhead annotation
exercises.instructions: Fill in: @Bulkhead(name='tracking', ___Method='cachedTracking'). Fallback: private TrackingUpdate cachedTracking(String id, ___FullException e)
hints: fallbackMethod: string attribute (same as other annotations)
BulkheadFullException: specific type thrown when bulkhead is saturated

exercises.title: Add @Bulkhead to limit concurrent search calls
exercises.instructions: FlipkartSearchService.search(): @Bulkhead('search', maxConcurrentCalls=50). Fallback returns empty list. application.yml config.
hints: BulkheadFullException: import io.github.resilience4j.bulkhead.BulkheadFullException
Empty list fallback: search degradation is acceptable
maxWaitDuration=100ms: wait briefly before giving up

exercises.title: Thread pool bulkhead for payment isolation
exercises.instructions: Write PaymentService with @Bulkhead(type=THREADPOOL): dedicated thread pool of 50 threads. Returns CompletableFuture<PaymentResult>. application.yml thread-pool-bulkhead config.
hints: type=THREADPOOL: dedicated thread pool, not semaphore
CompletableFuture: required return type for THREADPOOL bulkhead
queueCapacity: requests wait in queue before BulkheadFullException

exercises.title: Combine @Bulkhead + @CircuitBreaker
exercises.instructions: Write ZerodhaMarketDataService with @Bulkhead (outer, max 30) + @CircuitBreaker (inner). Explain: which rejects first when both limits are exceeded?
hints: @Bulkhead outer: concurrent slot check happens first
Two separate fallbackMethods: each annotation needs its own
BulkheadFullException vs Exception: use specific type for clarity

exercises.title: Read bulkhead metrics via BulkheadRegistry
exercises.instructions: Write BulkheadMonitor: inject BulkheadRegistry, log available permits and active calls for each bulkhead every 30 seconds.
hints: BulkheadRegistry: similar to CircuitBreakerRegistry
getAvailableConcurrentCalls(): permits not currently in use
80% utilization alert: proactive scaling signal

exercises.title: @SpringBootTest: verify bulkhead limits concurrent calls
exercises.instructions: Write a test: 5 concurrent threads call a @Bulkhead(max=3) method. Assert: exactly 3 complete normally, 2 receive fallback response. Use CountDownLatch for coordination.
hints: CountDownLatch for barrier: all threads start simultaneously for true concurrency test
allStarted.await(): synchronize all threads at the same moment
Success + fallback = total: all 5 threads complete (no exceptions escape)

exercises.title: Swiggy: feature isolation with multiple bulkheads
exercises.instructions: Write Swiggy OrderService with 3 separate bulkheads: order-tracking (50 max), restaurant-search (30 max), payment-processing (20 max). Each has appropriate fallback. Show: tracking saturation doesn't block payments.
hints: Three independent bulkheads: independent counters
Payment: longer maxWaitDuration (500ms) — worth waiting for a slot
Tracking fallback: return cached, not an error — degraded but functional

exercises.title: Bulkhead event listener for saturation alerting
exercises.instructions: Write BulkheadSaturationMonitor: register event listeners on all bulkheads. Track rejected call count. Alert if any bulkhead has > 100 rejections/minute.
hints: onCallRejected: event fired when BulkheadFullException would be thrown
getAndSet(0): rolling window — reset every minute
Alert: > 100 rejections/min means bulkhead is too small or feature is too slow

exercises.title: Semaphore vs thread pool bulkhead — which is right?
exercises.instructions:
For each scenario, state: semaphore or thread pool bulkhead, and why:
A: Order tracking: many concurrent, slow, non-critical
B: Payment processing: critical, must be isolated
C: Product search: fast DB query, limit concurrent
D: Report generation: takes 30 seconds, should not block caller
exercises.expected_solution:
A: SEMAPHORE — order tracking is high volume but the calling thread can block briefly.
   maxConcurrentCalls=100, fallback=cached data. Simple and low overhead.

B: SEMAPHORE or THREAD POOL — payments are critical.
   Thread Pool if you want true isolation (dedicated threads never shared).
   Semaphore if you just want to limit concurrency.
   Prefer THREADPOOL for complete isolation from other features.

C: SEMAPHORE — fast DB query, just limit concurrent calls to prevent DB saturation.
   maxConcurrentCalls=50, maxWaitDuration=100ms. No need for separate threads.

D: THREAD POOL — 30-second generation should not block the calling thread.
   THREADPOOL returns CompletableFuture immediately; caller gets the future.
   Without THREADPOOL: 500 concurrent report requests × 30s = 15,000 thread-seconds blocked.

test_cases.expected:
A: SEMAPHORE — order tracking is high volume but the calling thread can block briefly.
   maxConcurrentCalls=100, fallback=cached data. Simple and low overhead.

B: SEMAPHORE or THREAD POOL — payments are critical.
   Thread Pool if you want true isolation (dedicated threads never shared).
   Semaphore if you just want to limit concurrency.
   Prefer THREADPOOL for complete isolation from other features.

C: SEMAPHORE — fast DB query, just limit concurrent calls to prevent DB saturation.
   maxConcurrentCalls=50, maxWaitDuration=100ms. No need for separate threads.

D: THREAD POOL — 30-second generation should not block the calling thread.
   THREADPOOL returns CompletableFuture immediately; caller gets the future.
   Without THREADPOOL: 500 concurrent report requests × 30s = 15,000 thread-seconds blocked.

hints: THREADPOOL: use when caller should NOT wait (long-running operations)
SEMAPHORE: use when you just want to limit concurrency of an existing call
THREADPOOL requires CompletableFuture return type

exercises.title: Bulkhead with graceful degradation tiers
exercises.instructions: BookMyShow SeatService: if bulkhead full → return simplified zone pricing (not an error). If zone pricing also fails → return 'loading' placeholder. Two-tier degradation.
hints: Tier 2 fallback calls Tier 2 service: which also has a bulkhead
Three levels of degradation: interactive → zones → loading
Never return a hard error to the user: always have a final tier

exercises.title: Full Swiggy order service with all three bulkheads and tests
exercises.instructions: Build the complete SwiggyOrderService with bulkhead isolation and 2 @SpringBootTest tests verifying payments still work when tracking is saturated.
hints: Payment test: mock payment client return APPROVED, verify result
Bulkhead full concurrency test: use CountDownLatch for true parallel calls
Application.yml: swiggy-tracking (max=50), swiggy-payment (max=20)

exercises.title: Razorpay: complete isolation — bulkhead + CB + retry
exercises.instructions: Write complete RazorpayPaymentOrchestrator with all three patterns stacked in correct order: @Bulkhead (outer, max=50) → @CircuitBreaker → @Retry (inner). Three distinct fallbacks. Test: verify each layer's fallback fires appropriately.
hints: Three stacked annotations need three separate fallback methods
BulkheadFullException fallback specifically typed
Correct order: Bulkhead > CB > Retry (top to bottom in code)

exercises.title: Thread pool bulkhead for async report generation
exercises.instructions: BookMyShow ReportService: generateSalesReport() takes 30 seconds. THREADPOOL bulkhead (max 5 threads). Returns CompletableFuture. Test: 3 reports start concurrently, all complete asynchronously.
hints: CompletableFuture.allOf: wait for all futures to complete
Thread.sleep in mock: simulates report generation time
THREADPOOL: all 3 start immediately (not queued behind each other)

exercises.title: Complete Zerodha resilience synthesis — all 4 patterns
exercises.instructions: Build ZerodhaFullResilience combining: @Bulkhead + @CircuitBreaker + @Retry + @RateLimiter on the order submission path. All four patterns in correct order. Test (2 tests): retry succeeds on 3rd, fallback chain works.
hints: Order: RateLimiter > Bulkhead > CircuitBreaker > Retry (outermost to innermost)
Four separate fallback methods, each typed for its specific exception
Test 2: which fallback fires depends on which limit is hit first
